sourceMovie.title: Agent Cody Banks
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Agent Cody Banks for the same reason it broke through in 2003: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Harald Zwart keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Secret Headquarters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Agent Cody Banks clicked for you, Secret Headquarters (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Agent Cody Banks?
faq.answer: Try Secret Headquarters, The Man from U.N.C.L.E., Johnny English Strikes Again—each shares DNA with Agent Cody Banks while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Agent Cody Banks?
faq.answer: Agent Cody Banks (2003) is best described as Action, Adventure, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Agent Cody Banks part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Agent Cody Banks Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
